content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN and must construct a permutation PPP of integers [1,N][1, N][1,N].

You define f(P)f(P)f(P) as the number of integers iii (1‚â§i<N1 \le i < N1‚â§i<N) such that min‚Å°(Pi,Pi+1)\min(P_i, P_{i + 1})min(Pi‚Äã,Pi+1‚Äã) is prime. Note that 111 is not considered a prime integer. For example, f([4,2,1,6,5,3])=3f([4, 2, 1, 6, 5, 3]) = 3f([4,2,1,6,5,3])=3 because i=1,4,5i = 1, 4, 5i=1,4,5 satisfies the condition.

Find a permutation with minimum value of f(P)f(P)f(P). If multiple permutations exist, you may print any.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first and only line of input contains a single integer NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output a permutation P‚àí[P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PN]P - [P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_N]P‚àí[P1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã] of integers [1,N][1, N][1,N] with minimum value of f(P)f(P)f(P).


If multiple answers are possible, print any.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105

Test Case 2 : f([2,1,3])=0f([2, 1, 3]) = 0f([2,1,3])=0 as min‚Å°(1,2)=min‚Å°(1,3)=1\min(1, 2) = \min(1, 3) = 1min(1,2)=min(1,3)=1 which is not prime. [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] will not be accepted because min‚Å°(P2,P3)=2\min(P_2, P_3) = 2min(P2‚Äã,P3‚Äã)=2 which is prime.
